steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The sentence needs a plural subject (to match the plural verb "correlate") that also correctly shows possession (the lengths belong to the constitutions). Two things must be right: plural noun + correct possessive form.

steps.title: Apply plural agreement
steps.content:
Rule: plural possessive = noun + apostrophe after the s (constitutions'). Test each choice as the subject of "correlate":

A) constitutions' length, — possessive is correct (constitutions'), but "length" is singular. A singular noun cannot be the subject of the plural verb "correlate." Fails.

B) constitutions lengths, — no apostrophe at all, so no possessive relationship is shown between the constitutions and their lengths. Fails.

C) constitution's length, — singular possessive (one constitution's) and singular noun (length). Neither matches the plural verb nor the two constitutions described. Fails.

D) constitutions' lengths, — plural possessive (constitutions') plus plural noun (lengths). Creates a plural subject that correctly matches the plural verb "correlate." Correct.
